title: Embers, Complete
author: Parker, Gilbert
summary: Embers is a collected volume of lyrical poems by Gilbert Parker that gathers youthful memories, Irish ballads, travel impressions, and narrative lyrics. Voices range from soldiers and lovers to an introspective pilgrim who brings “one perfect yesterday” to an angel. Poems move through Inniskillen, Kildare, Donegal and distant lands, mixing dialect, song, and elegiac reflection. Themes include love and loss, homecoming, memory’s consolation, and the small rituals of village life. The book pairs popular singable ballads with more contemplative pieces—Proem and epigraph framing memory as the poet’s chief possession amid transient worldly travail and modest irony about fame and sorrow.

characters.name: The Angel
characters.lifespan: eternal; questions the pilgrim about his life in the Proem

characters.name: The Man (Proem)
characters.lifespan: survivor of experience; brings one perfect yesterday as his chief possession

characters.name: Rosleen
characters.lifespan: leaves the narrator for another; remains an object of longing

characters.name: Mary Callaghan
characters.lifespan: marries and becomes mother; celebrated in the wedding and christening poems

characters.name: Clancy
characters.lifespan: marries in the church scene; role in community event

characters.name: Father Tryon
characters.lifespan: officiates at Mary Callaghan's wedding; present in community rites

characters.name: The Sergeant
characters.lifespan: recruits or tempts narrator with a shilling; figure in departure from home

characters.name: Black Care
characters.personality: feared or notorious (name suggests personified trouble)
characters.lifespan: is said to have been killed by the man praised in 'Kildare'

characters.name: Lord Lieutenant
characters.lifespan: orders drums for celebration in 'Kildare'; appears as a civic figure

characters.name: The Gossoon
characters.lifespan: appears as Mary Callaghan's young child in the christening scene

characters.name: Narrator ('me')
characters.lifespan: speaks in first person across many pieces; survives as the vantage voice

characters.name: W. E. H.
characters.lifespan: named in contents; exact role unspecified in excerpt

characters.name: Mr. Max Muller
characters.lifespan: sets 'Mary Callaghan and Me' to music (mentioned in introduction)

characters.name: Sir Edward Elgar
characters.lifespan: composed settings for several of the poet's pieces (mentioned in introduction)

characters.name: Sir Alexander Mackenzie
characters.lifespan: credited with musical settings (mentioned in introduction)

characters.name: Arthur Foote
characters.lifespan: arranged 'You'll Travel Far and Wide' and brought it fame (mentioned in introduction)

characters.name: Mrs. Amy Woodforde Finden
characters.lifespan: set 'O Flower of All the World' to music (mentioned in introduction)

characters.name: Robert Somerville
characters.lifespan: mentioned as having set pieces to music (introduction)
